reasons: Field audit evaluated 11/45 Blue Origin launches with official source pages from the existing audit snapshot.
Mission-profile signals were present on 2/11 audited launches, timeline signals on 8/11, recovery signals on 8/11, and numeric mission facts on 0/11.
Only 0/11 audited launches carried both a numeric mission fact or orbit-like value and mission-profile or timeline structure, so usable coverage remains "no".

launches.name: New Glenn | Blue Moon Pathfinder Mission 2 & VIPER
launches.missionSummary: 2nd of the National Team’s Blue Moon MK1 lunar lander test missions to validate the necessary technologies for its HLS lunar module. Some of the life support hardware will travel on this mission in preparation for the first crew Blue Moon flight. This mission will also carry NASA’s VIPER (Volatiles Investigating Polar Exploration Rover) lunar rover. VIPER is designed to explore

launches.name: New Glenn | Blue Moon Pathfinder
launches.missionSummary: First flight of Blue Origin's Blue Moon MK1 lunar lander, MK1-SN001 "Endurance".

launches.name: New Glenn | BlueBird Block 2 #4
launches.missionSummary: AST SpaceMobile’s Block 2 BlueBird satellites are designed to deliver up to 10 times the bandwidth capacity of the BlueBird Block 1 satellites, required to achieve 24/7 continuous cellular broadband service coverage in the United States, with beams designed to support a capacity of up to 40 MHz, enabling peak data transmission speeds up to 120 Mbps, supporting voice, full data

launches.name: New Glenn | Amazon Leo (New Glenn #1)
launches.missionSummary: Amazon Leo, formerly known as Project Kuiper, is a mega constellation of satellites in Low Earth Orbit that will offer broadband internet access, this constellation will be managed by Kuiper Systems LLC, a subsidiary of Amazon. This constellation is planned to be composed of 3,276 satellites. The satellites are projected to be placed in 98 orbital planes in three orbital layers

launches.name: New Glenn | BlueBird Block 2 #2
launches.missionSummary: AST SpaceMobile’s Block 2 BlueBird satellites are designed to deliver up to 10 times the bandwidth capacity of the BlueBird Block 1 satellites, required to achieve 24/7 continuous cellular broadband service coverage in the United States, with beams designed to support a capacity of up to 40 MHz, enabling peak data transmission speeds up to 120 Mbps, supporting voice, full data

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-38
launches.missionSummary: NS-38 is the 17th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 38th in the New Shepard program's history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-37
launches.missionSummary: NS-37 is the 16th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 37th in the New Shepard program's history.

launches.name: New Glenn | EscaPADE
launches.missionSummary: New Glenn Mission NG-2 The New Glenn orbital launch vehicle successfully completed its second mission on November 13, 2025, deploying NASA’s Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers (ESCAPADE) twin-spacecraft into the designated loiter orbit, and landing the fully reusable first stage on Jacklyn in the Atlantic Ocean.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-36
launches.missionSummary: NS-36 is the 15th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 36th in the New Shepard program's history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-35
launches.missionSummary: NS-35 is the 35th flight for the New Shepard program. This flight will fly more than 40 scientific and research payloads to space and back, including 24 experiments from NASA’s TechRise Student Challenge and payloads for Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory, Oklahoma State University, University of Florida, Carthage College, University of Central Florida, Teledyne, Space La

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-34
launches.missionSummary: NS-34 is the 14th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 34th in its history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-33
launches.missionSummary: NS-33 is the 13th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 33rd in its history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-32
launches.missionSummary: NS-32 is the 12th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 32nd in its history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-31
launches.missionSummary: NS-31 is the 11th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 31st in its history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-30
launches.missionSummary: NS-30 is the 10th crewed flight for the New Shepard program and the 30th in its history.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-29
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed its 29th New Shepard flight and 14th payload mission. The payloads experienced roughly two minutes of lunar gravity forces.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin Completes 29th New Shepard Mission, Successfully Simulates Lunar Gravity | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Glenn | Maiden Flight
launches.missionSummary: Maiden flight of the New Glenn launch vehicle. Will carry the prototype Blue Ring payload tug and payload hoisting platform, consisting of communications array, power systems, and a flight computer affixed to a secondary payload adapter ring, remaining attached to the 2nd stage. This launch will serve as New Glenn's first National Security Space Launch certification flight. Tar

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-28
launches.missionSummary: Twenty-eighth flight of New Shepard carrying six passengers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-27
launches.missionSummary: Twenty-seventh flight of New Shepard, flying without crew as a test for the newest booster.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-26
launches.missionSummary: Twenty-sixth flight of New Shepard carrying six passengers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-25
launches.missionSummary: Twenty-fifth flight of New Shepard carrying six passengers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-24
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed its 24th New Shepard flight and 13th payload mission today from Launch Site One in West Texas.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin Successfully Completes 24th Mission to Space | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-23
launches.missionSummary: Twenty-third flight of New Shepard carrying 36 science and research payloads along with tens of thousands of postcards from Club For Future.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-22
launches.missionSummary: NS-22 is the sixth crewed New Shepard flight, carrying six passengers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-21
launches.missionSummary: Today, Blue Origin successfully completed its fifth human spaceflight and the 21st flight for the New Shepard program.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin Successfully Completes 21st Mission | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-20
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed its fourth human spaceflight and 20th overall flight for New Shepard.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin Successfully Completes Fourth Human Spaceflight | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-19
launches.missionSummary: NS-19 is the third crewed New Shepard flight, carrying two honorary guests and four paying customers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-18
launches.missionSummary: NS-18 is the second crewed New Shepard flight, carrying 4 paying customers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-17
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed the 17th New Shepard mission to space and back for the program, and the 8th consecutive flight for this particular vehicle.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: New Shepard Mission NS-17 Launch Updates | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-16
launches.missionSummary: First crewed flight of Blue Origin's New Shepard rocket.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-15
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed its 15th consecutive mission to space and back. This mission marked a verification step prior to flying astronauts.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin Conducts Astronaut Rehearsal for Future Customer Flights | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-14
launches.missionSummary: Blue Origin successfully completed its 14th mission to space and back today for the New Shepard program.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Mission NS-14 successfully demonstrates crew capsule upgrades | Blue Origin

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-13
launches.missionSummary: This will be the 13th New Shepard mission and the 7th consecutive flight for this particular vehicle (a record), demonstrating its operational reusability. New Shepard will fly 12 commercial payloads to space and back on this mission, including the Deorbit, Descent, and Landing Sensor Demonstration with NASA’s Space Technology Mission Directorate under a Tipping Point partnersh

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-12
launches.missionSummary: The 12th flight of New Shepard will contain commercial payloads and postcards from the nonprofit organisation 'Club for the Future'.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-11
launches.missionSummary: New Shepard flight 11 will fly 38 microgravity research payloads to space.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-10
launches.missionSummary: Earth, in all its beauty, is just our starting place. Blue Origin is opening the promise of space to all.
launches.selectedSourceTitle: Blue Origin | New Shepard to fly 9 NASA-sponsored Payloads to Space on NS-10

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-9
launches.missionSummary: The third commercial flight for Blue Origin. NS-9 carried various payloads on a sub-orbital flight. It also tested the capsule escape rockets at high altitude.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-8
launches.missionSummary: NS-8 is Blue Origin's second commercial flight. It carried payloads from various customers on a sub-orbital launch for 3 minutes of microgravity.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-7
launches.missionSummary: NS-7 is Blue Origins first commercial flight. The payloads included 12 experiments conducted in 3 minutes of microgravity dedicated to medical and earth science.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-6
launches.missionSummary: The 6th flight for Blue Origin, NS-6 will test an in-flight abort scenario for the capsule.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-5
launches.missionSummary: Fifth flight for Blue Origin. The NS-5 mission successfully landed the core stage and tested a capsule landing with one parachute cut.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-4
launches.missionSummary: The fourth flight for Blue Origin. The capsule reached an apogee of 103.8 kilometers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-3
launches.missionSummary: The third test flight of the New Shepard vehicle. It is the first re-use for Blue Origin.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-2
launches.missionSummary: The first flight of the New Shepard 2 booster. The capsule reached an apogee of 100.5 kilometers.

launches.name: New Shepard | NS-1
launches.missionSummary: The first flight of the New Shepard 1 booster. The capsule reached an apogee of 93.5 kilometers.
